title: Flutter Codebase Semantic Index
description: Persistent, incremental map of a Flutter app's symbols, produced by scripts/build_index.py. Skills QUERY this instead of re-reading lib/** — context cost becomes O(change), not O(app). House style: ../../../references/CONVENTIONS.md.

schemaVersion.description: Index format version. Bump when entity shapes change so consumers can detect a stale schema and trigger a full rebuild.

generatedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the index was written.

root.description: Project root, relative to the index file (always '.', since the index lives at <root>/.flutter-pipeline/index.json).

package.description: Dart package name from pubspec.yaml. Used to resolve `package:<name>/...` imports into lib/-relative paths for the dependency edge list.

counts.description: Entity counts by kind — a cheap header for skills to read before deciding whether to load the full entity list.

entities.description: Flat list of every extracted symbol. This is the primary query surface.

files.description: Per-file dependency edges (file -> internal files it imports) plus the mtime used for incremental rebuilds. The file-level `dependsOn` is the source of truth; each entity copies its file's edges for query convenience.

mtime.description: Source file mtime (epoch seconds) at scan time. Drives --incremental.

dependsOn.description: lib/-relative paths of project-internal files imported by this file. External `package:` imports are excluded.

_meta.description: Build diagnostics (not part of the query contract).

reused.description: Files reused from the prior index (incremental).

entity.description: One indexed symbol. `id` is globally unique; `kind` discriminates the shape.

id.description: Stable unique id. Shape: '<kind>:<file>#<name>' for file-owned entities, 'feature:<name>' for synthesized features. Use as a graph node id (e.g. when handing off to graphify).

kind.description: Entity discriminator. See per-kind notes below.

name.description: Symbol name. For provider = the *provider* name (e.g. 'authStateProvider'); for route = its name (or path if unnamed); for feature = the directory name.

file.description: lib/-relative POSIX path of the declaring file. For 'feature' this is the feature directory (trailing slash).

line.description: 1-based line number of the declaration — lets a skill jump straight to it (Read with offset) instead of scanning the file.

feature.description: Owning feature (lib/features/<feature>/...) when the entity lives under a feature. Absent for app/core/shared code.

dependsOn.description: File-level edges copied from this entity's file: the lib/-relative files it imports. Enables 'what does X depend on / what depends on X' queries without re-reading.

base.description: SCREEN only. The widget superclass: ConsumerWidget | StatelessWidget | StatefulWidget | ConsumerStatefulWidget | HookConsumerWidget | HookWidget.

style.description: PROVIDER: how it's declared — 'codegen-class' (@riverpod class extends _$X), 'codegen-fn' (@riverpod function), or 'manual' (final xProvider = ...). MODEL: 'freezed'.

notifier.description: PROVIDER (codegen-class) only. The Notifier/AsyncNotifier class name the provider is generated from.

function.description: PROVIDER (codegen-fn) only. The annotated function name (provider name is '<function>Provider').

providerType.description: PROVIDER (manual) only. The right-hand provider constructor, e.g. 'Provider', 'StreamProvider', 'StateNotifierProvider.autoDispose'.

path.description: ROUTE only. The go_router path. For RouteDef-table routes this is the literal ('/login', ':id'); for inline GoRoutes with a literal path it's that path. References like AppRoute.x.path are resolved against the RouteDef table when possible.

routeVar.description: ROUTE (from RouteDef table) only. The static const variable name in routes.dart, e.g. 'itemDetail'.

source.description: ROUTE only. 'GoRoute' when extracted from an inline GoRoute(...) with a literal path; absent when from the RouteDef table.

role.description: REPOSITORY only. 'interface' = abstract (interface) class XRepository; 'impl' = class XRepositoryImpl implements XRepository.

implements.description: REPOSITORY (role=impl) only. The interface it implements/extends, linking impl -> interface.
